events.title: Iran war day 73: Trump and Tehran clash over latest peace proposals
events.desc: Tensions remain high as President Donald Trump has rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, describing it as 'totally unacceptable'. Brent crude prices have surged amid concerns that the 10-week-old conflict will continue, leading to a prolonged paralysis of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Trump rejects Iran's response to US peace proposal as 'unacceptable'
events.desc: President Donald Trump has swiftly rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, causing oil prices to surge on concerns that the 10-week-old conflict will prolong, keeping shipping through the Strait of Hormuz paralyzed. Iran is reportedly seeking the lifting of the U.S. naval blockade, recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and compensation for war damages.

events.title: South Korea condemns attack on cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz, vows response
events.desc: South Korea has strongly condemned an attack on a cargo ship operated by a Korean shipper in the Strait of Hormuz and vowed to respond once the source of the attack is identified. An initial forensic analysis of the damage indicated a fire in the vessel's engine room.

events.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes killed 39
events.desc: Israel and Hezbollah have continued to exchange fire, resulting in significant casualties. In a recent development, Israeli strikes in Lebanon have reportedly killed 39 people, underscoring the ongoing escalation in hostilities despite a ceasefire deal announced last month.

events.title: Iran war 'not over,' uranium must be removed: Netanyahu
events.desc: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that the US-Israeli war against Iran is not over as long as enriched uranium remains within Iran. He emphasized that stockpiles of enriched uranium must be 'taken out' and enrichment sites dismantled.

events.title: Middle East conflicts a danger for whales off S.Africa: study
events.desc: Conflicts in the Middle East are posing increased dangers to whales off the coast of South Africa. The rerouting of shipping traffic away from the Red Sea and Suez Canal due to these conflicts has shifted sea traffic into whale habitats, significantly raising the risk of collisions.

events.title: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
events.desc: A British couple, Lindsay and Craig Foreman, face the reality of a 10-year prison sentence in Iran following their arrest while on a motorcycle tour. They acknowledge that their prolonged detention is highly probable.

events.title: UK, France to host defence ministers meeting on Hormuz
events.desc: The UK and France will convene a multinational meeting of defense ministers to discuss military strategies for restoring trade flow through the Strait of Hormuz. This initiative comes after Iran warned both nations against sending warships to the region.

events.title: Trump and China's Xi set for talks spanning Iran, nuclear, trade and AI
events.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are scheduled to discuss critical issues including Iran, Taiwan, artificial intelligence, and nuclear weapons. The talks will also cover the potential extension of a critical minerals deal, as the two leaders aim to stabilize strained bilateral ties.

events.title: As Adversaries Integrate, U.S. Partners Bypass Washington
events.desc: Iranian drones, enhanced by Russian technology and battlefield testing, are targeting Gulf Arab states. The U.S. finds itself in an unfamiliar position, requesting counter-drone assistance from Ukraine to defend against these sophisticated Iranian attacks.

events.title: The Global Sumud Flotilla is sailing on, here is why
events.desc: Despite an Israeli attack on a portion of the Global Sumud Flotilla, organizers have stated their determination to continue the mission, underscoring their commitment to their objectives.

events.title: Ankara’s Crossroads: Rearmament, Risk, and the Prospect of War with Israel
events.desc: Concerns are rising about a potential conflict between Israel and Turkey, driven by issues related to Syria, the Palestinian territories, and regional security. These tensions were highlighted by Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu's indirect charges that Ankara seeks to reestablish Ottoman imperial rule over the Levant.

events.title: Practice Makes Deterrence: India’s Next Nuclear Challenge at Sea
events.desc: India's nuclear deterrent capabilities have significantly advanced with the commissioning of its third ballistic missile-carrying, nuclear-powered submarine, the INS Aridhaman. This development ensures India can maintain a consistent deterrent patrol and potentially deploy multiple independently targetable warheads, enhancing its second-strike capability.

events.title: “Make Iran Ungovernable” – Tracking Efforts To Destroy Iran’s Police Infrastructure
events.desc: In the initial three weeks of the US and Israeli war against Iran, at least 80 police stations and related infrastructure, including Basij paramilitary sites, have been damaged or destroyed. Experts suggest this is a deliberate effort by both countries to degrade the Iranian regime's repressive capacity.

events.title: Two Waves of Bombing: New Videos Reveal Further Details About Iran School Strike
events.desc: Bellingcat has verified and geolocated two new videos depicting the strikes on an IRGC compound and an adjacent school in Minab in late February. The videos, released by Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, show multiple missile impacts on the complex.
